summarization.a: Side view of the A.2-O:245 configuration showing a triply bonded (TB) TMPS molecule attached to silanol positions O2, O4, and O5 on the A.2 amorphous silica surface. The free silanol at O6 and the reactive bridge oxygen (RBO) species are labeled. The TB configuration requires significant framework distortion due to the large inter-silanol distances on A.2.
summarization.b: Side view of the A.1-O:123/56/4 full coverage configuration on the A.1 surface. This shows three SMIs: a TB-TMPS at positions O1, O2, O3; a DB-TMPS at positions O5, O6; and an SB-TMPS at position O4. All six surface silanols are blocked by this arrangement.
summarization.c: Top view of the A.2-O:245 configuration, showing the spatial arrangement of the TB-TMPS bonding to three silanols (O2, O4, O5), the free silanol O6, and the RBO beneath the blocking layer.
summarization.d: Top view of the A.1-O:123/56/4 configuration, illustrating how three SMIs achieve full silanol coverage on the A.1 surface. The numbered positions show the silanol bonding sites for each SMI.

d.question: Which configuration provides better blocking layer coverage and why?
d.answer: The A.1-O:123/56/4 configuration provides superior coverage because it blocks all six surface silanols using three SMIs in a combination of TB, DB, and SB bonding modes. In contrast, A.2-O:245 leaves O6 unblocked and retains a reactive RBO species beneath the layer. The unblocked silanol and RBO in A.2-O:245 represent potential nucleation sites for Al precursors, which would reduce AS-ALD selectivity.

c.question: Why does the A.2-O:245 configuration leave O6 as a free silanol?
c.answer: The large inter-silanol distances on A.2 prevent formation of stable multiply bonded configurations that would include O6, and the existing TB-TMPS at O2,O4,O5 sterically blocks access to O6 by additional TMPS molecules.

a.question: What is the RBO species visible in panels (a) and (c)?
a.answer: RBO stands for Reactive Bridge Oxygen, a strained Si-O-Si surface species that forms when two adjacent silanols condense. It is highly reactive toward both Al precursors and water.

b.question: What does the configuration name "A.1-O:123/56/4" indicate?
b.answer: The naming convention encodes the surface model and SMI bonding arrangement. "A.1" refers to the silanol-rich amorphous silica model with six surface silanols. The numbers after "O:" indicate which silanol positions each SMI occupies, separated by slashes for different SMI molecules. Thus "123/56/4" means one SMI bonds to O1, O2, and O3 (triply bonded), another bonds to O5 and O6 (doubly bonded), and a third bonds only to O4 (singly bonded).
